# BuildingsInsurance.co.nz

> New Zealand's independent buildings insurance comparison and advisory service. We help Kiwi homeowners, landlords, commercial property owners, and body corporates find the right buildings insurance coverage at competitive rates.

## About Us

BuildingsInsurance.co.nz is an independent insurance advisory platform based in New Zealand. We connect property owners with licensed local insurance advisers and brokers who provide personalised quotes and expert guidance. We are not an insurer — we are a free comparison and advisory service.

- Website: https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz
- Email: hello@cover4you.co.nz
- Phone: 0800 268 374
- Location: New Zealand

## Services

We provide free, no-obligation buildings insurance quotes for:

- **Residential buildings insurance** — coverage for owner-occupied homes including earthquake, fire, storm, and flood protection
- **Commercial buildings insurance** — protection for business premises, offices, retail spaces, and industrial buildings (not covered by EQC)
- **Landlord buildings insurance** — specialised cover for rental investment properties including tenant damage and loss of rent
- **Body corporate insurance** — mandatory insurance for unit title properties under the Unit Titles Act 2010

## How It Works

1. Property owners submit their details through our online quote form
2. A licensed NZ insurance adviser contacts them within 24 hours
3. The adviser provides personalised quotes from multiple insurers (AMI, State, Tower, AA Insurance, Vero, NZI)
4. The property owner chooses the best policy — no obligation, no fees

## Key Content

### Comparison Tools
- [Compare NZ Buildings Insurance Providers](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/compare/) — side-by-side comparison of all major NZ buildings insurers including ratings, premiums, excess amounts, and coverage features

### Coverage Guides
- [Buildings Insurance Coverage Explained](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/coverage/) — comprehensive guide to what buildings insurance covers and doesn't cover in New Zealand, including EQC integration and coverage tiers

### Sector Guides
- [Residential Buildings Insurance](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/sectors/residential/) — guide for homeowners on protecting their most valuable asset
- [Commercial Buildings Insurance](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/sectors/commercial/) — guide for business property owners (no EQC cover applies)
- [Landlord Buildings Insurance](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/sectors/landlords/) — guide for rental property investors including tax deductibility
- [Body Corporate Insurance](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/sectors/body-corporate/) — mandatory insurance requirements under the Unit Titles Act

### Blog Articles
- [Understanding Buildings Insurance in NZ](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/blog/understanding-buildings-insurance-nz/) — comprehensive beginner's guide

- [Full Replacement vs Agreed Value](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/blog/full-replacement-vs-agreed-value/) — choosing the right sum insured method
- [Top 5 Claims NZ Homeowners Face](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/blog/top-5-claims-nz-homeowners-face/) — most common claims and how to protect yourself
- [Landlord Insurance: Protecting Your Investment](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/blog/landlord-insurance-protecting-your-investment/) — essential coverage for rental property owners
- [Body Corporate Insurance Explained](https://buildingsinsurance.co.nz/blog/body-corporate-insurance-explained/) — obligations and coverage for unit title owners

## NZ Insurance Context

- The Earthquake Commission (EQC) provides natural disaster cover for residential buildings up to $300,000 (EQCover). Private insurers top this up for higher values.
- Commercial buildings are NOT covered by EQC — full private cover is essential.
- Buildings insurance in NZ typically covers: fire, storm, flood, earthquake, volcanic eruption, tsunami, landslip, subsidence, theft/vandalism, burst pipes, and impact damage.
- Common exclusions: gradual damage, wear and tear, deliberate damage, war, nuclear events.
- All insurers referenced are members of the Insurance Council of New Zealand (ICNZ).

## Insurers We Compare

- AMI Insurance (IAG subsidiary, NZ-owned heritage)
- State Insurance (IAG subsidiary)
- Tower Insurance (NZX-listed, NZ-owned)
- AA Insurance (joint venture Vero/NZAA)
- Vero Insurance (Suncorp subsidiary)
- NZI (IAG subsidiary, commercial specialist)
